skill.name: systematic-debugging
skill.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Four-phase framework ensuring understanding before attempting solutions.
skill.summary: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Four-ph...
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged fenced Markdown blocks in SKILL.md as shell backtick execution, but the reviewed lines contain debugging instructions and templates, not executable code. The weak cryptography and entropy alerts are also false positives; no cryptographic implementation, obfuscated payload, network access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at multiple SKILL.md locations. These lines are fenced Markdown examples and Korean-language debugging checklists, not code that executes commands. The skill does declare Bash as an allowed tool, so command use should remain user-directed and evidence-based.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fences containing prose, lists, and a debugging template. I found no executable Ruby, shell interpolation, or command strings in those blocks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3. That line is the skill description and contains no hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or security-sensitive algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain frontmatter text describing the debugging workflow. There is no cryptographic code or configuration to exploit.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported high entropy at SKILL.md line 1. The file is readable Markdown with YAML frontmatter and Korean text, and I found no binary blob, encoded payload, encrypted content, or hidden instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed normal Markdown content throughout the file. The entropy signal is plausibly caused by multilingual text and formatting, not obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static External Command Pattern Dismissed
dangerous_patterns.description: Thirteen static external command detections map to Markdown code fences and template text in SKILL.md. They do not contain executable command invocations, but the skill's allowed Bash capability is noted as a low-risk operational factor.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Every reported external command location is part of Markdown documentation. No semantic evidence of command injection, data exfiltration, or automated destructive action was found.

content.user_title: Debug Bugs with a Root Cause Framework
content.value_statement: Debugging often fails when teams patch symptoms before understanding the failure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a four-phase root cause workflow.
actual_capabilities: Guides bug investigation through root cause, pattern analysis, hypothesis testing, and implementation phases.
Encourages exact error capture, reproduction steps, recent change review, and data flow tracing.
Provides a reusable debugging report template for documenting symptoms, hypotheses, tests, and results.
Promotes one-change-at-a-time fixes and validation after each attempted solution.
Defines stop conditions when repeated fixes fail and architecture review may be needed.
limitations: It is a methodology guide, not an automated debugger or tracing engine.
It does not include scripts, integrations, or project-specific diagnostics.
It depends on accurate logs, reproduction steps, and context from the user.
It may slow very small fixes where the root cause is already proven.

use_cases.title: Investigate Reproducible Bugs
use_cases.description: Use the phases to capture symptoms, reproduce the issue, compare working examples, and test one hypothesis at a time.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Failing Tests
use_cases.description: Document the exact failure, identify recent changes, isolate the failing behavior, and apply one verified correction.

use_cases.title: Structure Production Incident Analysis
use_cases.description: Organize evidence, trace data flow across components, and avoid premature fixes during incident response.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Bug Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use systematic-debugging to investigate this bug. First help me capture the exact symptom, reproduction steps, expected result, and actual result before proposing fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Test Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use systematic-debugging on this failing test. Compare it with a passing case, identify the meaningful differences, and form one testable hypothesis.

prompt_templates.title: Trace a Multi-Component Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use systematic-debugging for this multi-service issue. Trace the data flow backward from the failure and list the evidence needed at each boundary.

prompt_templates.title: Review Failed Fix Attempts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use systematic-debugging to review these three failed fixes. Decide whether the current approach should stop and whether an architecture-level cause is likely.

output_examples.input: A user reports that checkout sometimes fails after payment authorization.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a structured investigation with exact symptoms, reproduction gaps, recent checkout changes, payment-to-order data flow, and one hypothesis to test first.

output_examples.input: A CI test started failing after a dependency update.
output_examples.output: The assistant compares the failing test with passing examples, isolates the changed dependency behavior, proposes a minimal verification, and delays code changes until evidence supports them.

output_examples.input: Two quick fixes did not resolve a production error.
output_examples.output: The assistant documents attempted fixes, checks whether each hypothesis was disproven, and recommends stopping broad changes until the system boundary is reviewed.

best_practices: Collect logs, stack traces, inputs, and reproduction steps before changing code.
Change only one variable at a time and record the result.
Stop after repeated failed fixes and reassess the system design or assumptions.
anti_patterns: Guessing a fix before reproducing the bug.
Applying several changes at once without isolating cause and effect.
Ignoring failed hypotheses and continuing the same repair strategy.

faq.question: Does this skill run debugging commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a debugging process and may guide tool use, but it does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it fix bugs by itself?
faq.answer: It can help plan and validate a fix after evidence supports a root cause. It is not a guarantee of repair.

faq.question: Why does it require root cause analysis first?
faq.answer: Root cause analysis reduces symptom fixes and helps prevent repeated failures from the same underlying issue.

faq.question: Is it useful for intermittent bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes reproduction evidence, recent changes, and data flow tracing, which are important for intermittent failures.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the error message, observed behavior, expected behavior, reproduction steps, recent changes, and relevant logs.
